name: dbmate (Database Migration Tool)

agent_readiness.notes: Language-agnostic SQL database migration tool. export DATABASE_URL='postgres://user:pass@localhost/mydb'. dbmate new create_users_table → creates db/migrations/20260309120000_create_users_table.sql. Migration file has -- migrate:up / -- migrate:down sections. dbmate up → run pending migrations. dbmate down → rollback. dbmate rollback → rollback last. dbmate status → pending migrations. dbmate dump → dump schema. dbmate create → create database. dbmate drop → drop database. Supports: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQLite, ClickHouse. Schema tracking in schema_migrations table. Single binary with no dependencies. CI-friendly. Works with any programming language or framework. Migrations are plain SQL.

gotchas: Plain SQL only — no DSL for generating migrations from models
Rollbacks require manually writing -- migrate:down section
No automatic schema change detection from models (unlike Alembic)
DATABASE_URL must be set — no config file for credentials
best_when: Language-agnostic projects needing simple SQL migrations
Teams wanting plain SQL without ORM coupling
Polyglot stacks where multiple languages share one DB
CI/CD pipelines needing a single binary migration runner
avoid_when: When auto-generated migrations from ORM models are needed (use Alembic)
Complex rollback scenarios requiring programmatic logic
When an ORM-specific migration tool already handles the workflow
summary: dbmate is a language-agnostic SQL migration tool. DATABASE_URL=postgres://... dbmate up/down/rollback/status. Migrations as plain .sql files with -- migrate:up / -- migrate:down. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, ClickHouse. Single Go binary, no deps. MIT. Best for polyglot stacks or plain SQL migrations without ORM coupling. No auto-generated migrations from models. CI-friendly single binary.
